name: Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp soy sauce, tamari, or Bragg's liquid aminos
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the spring roll wrappers: Place them flat and handle gently. Do not substitute egg roll wrappers.
2. Slice tofu into 1/2 inch slices, pat dry with paper towels, and press for an hour or so if possible. Add soy sauce to the tofu slices and bake at 325°F (160°C) for about 45 minutes, turning occasionally and adding more soy sauce as needed until brown and dry. Cut into strips.
3. Wash and dry lettuce; tear into 3-4 inch pieces, removing stems and crisp veins. Lettuce should be limp.
4. Wash mint leaves, remove stems, and set aside. Use fresh cilantro if mint unavailable.
5. Shred or grate carrots finely. Do not make them crisp.
6. Cook rice vermicelli in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then rinse with cool water. Drain well and let cool.
7. Prepare the filling: Place lettuce, mint, noodles, tofu strip, and carrot pieces onto a spring roll wrapper. Fold the bottom over the fillings, then sides, and continue rolling until complete. Wrap each roll in plastic wrap to prevent drying.
8. Assemble remaining rolls similarly.
9. Serve cold or at room temperature with desired sauces. Do not cook the rolls; they are ready when rolled up. Refrigerate for a day or two and serve 1-2 per person as an appetizer, or 3+ per person as a main course.
10. Sweet sauce: Combine sugar, soy sauce, broth (or water), cornstarch mixed with cold water, garlic, and salt in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, add cornstarch mixture, simmer for 1 minute, stir in garlic, and serve at any temperature.
